science.prompt: Which ancient serpent ancestor was really showing off its smarts and habitat preferences?

A fossil snake from the Late Cretaceous of Brazil

science.prompt: How do fruit flies navigate a smell? With memory, or just winging it?

answers.text: They hire a bloodhound to sniff out the buffet
They follow a tiny GPS chip implanted by scientists
A fossil snake from the Late Cretaceous of Brazil
They use memory mechanisms to track a smell's source

science.summary: Experiments in Drosophila show that flies use memory mechanisms to navigate along the boundary of an odour plume and track the source of a smell.

It sent back a postcard saying 'Wish you were here'
Flies used memory to find their way
It started a podcast about life in the Kuiper Belt

science.summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft awoke from a 321-day hibernation in good health to resume unprecedented science at the solar system's edge.

science.prompt: What happened to the Milky Way after a cosmic fender bender 10 billion years ago?

It got a new bumper sticker reading 'My other galaxy is a dwarf'
It may have been flipped by more than 90 degrees
It broke into a disco spin move that made all stars dizzy

science.summary: A collision between the Milky Way and a dwarf galaxy 10 billion years ago may have flipped the Milky Way by more than 90 degrees.

science.prompt: Which unlikely culprit made Earth's 'Great Dying' even worse?

answers.text: The Milky Way flipped over from a collision
An unexpected plant made the extinction worse
A self-help guru told the dinosaurs to 'let go' and they did
A rogue band of prehistoric beavers dammed all the rivers

science.summary: Some 200 million years ago, dramatic changes in climate and geology drove the extinction of approximately 80% of species on Earth.
